perturbation.original_text: Subject to the provisions of Section 14 hereof, the term of this Agreement and the sponsorship described herein shall commence on January 1, 2010 and extend through December 31, 2010.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the provisions of Section 14 hereof, the term of this Agreement and the sponsorship described herein shall commence on January 1, 2010 and extend through December 31, 2010, unless terminated earlier as per the discretion outlined in Section 9.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'unless terminated earlier as per the discretion outlined in Section 9' introduces a contradiction. While Section 1 states the agreement extends through December 31, 2010, the added phrase suggests Section 9 gives discretionary termination rights that could shorten the term regardless of section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Racing and all Racing members, including but not limited to all drivers, agree to use best efforts to conduct themselves in such a manner so as not to reflect unfavorably upon the Sponsor or its products. The Sponsor shall have the right to terminate this Agreement on written notice to Racing if any driver, the general manager or any other member of Racing (i) fails to conduct himself/herself in accordance with generally accepted standards of morality, (ii) engages in any activity which reflects adversely on the image, reputation or goodwill of the Sponsor or (iii) disparages the products or services of the Sponsor; provided, however, the Sponsor shall not have the right to terminate this Agreement if Racing, within fifteen (15) days after receipt of written notice by the Sponsor terminates the employment of, or otherwise dismisses from the racing team, the driver(s), general manager(s) or other member(s) of Racing engaging in the offensive conduct. Upon termination, the Sponsor shall be entitled to a pro rata refund of monies paid for services not yet performed by Racing based upon the number of races for the applicable racing season. The Sponsor's decision with respect to all matters arising under this Section shall be conclusive.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a fixed term ending December 31, 2010. The changed text introduces an exception allowing for earlier termination at the discretion outlined in Section 9. This directly contradicts the fixed term stated in the original text because Section 9 allows the Sponsor to terminate the agreement under certain conditions (e.g., a driver's misconduct), which could occur before December 31, 2010. This is a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications regarding the term of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Racing agrees that, during the term of this Agreement, (i) Sponsor shall have the right to approve or disapprove any additional sponsor identified by Racing, and (ii) unless another proposed sponsor has agreed to pay a sponsorship fee that exceeds the amount paid by Sponsor, no other sponsor shall receive any benefit of greater value (including either an equivalent or a more prominent use of another sponsor's name, logo or other identifying information) than the Benefits provided to the Sponsor hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Racing agrees that, during the term of this Agreement, (i) Sponsor shall have the right to approve or disapprove any additional sponsor identified by Racing, and (ii) Racing can secure benefits of greater value to other sponsors as long as the additional sponsorship does not conflict directly with Gainsco's marketing efforts. Unless another proposed sponsor has agreed to pay a sponsorship fee that exceeds the amount paid by Sponsor, no other sponsor shall receive any benefit of greater value (including either an equivalent or a more prominent use of another sponsor's name, logo or other identifying information) than the Benefits provided to the Sponsor hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'Racing can secure benefits of greater value to other sponsors as long as the additional sponsorship does not conflict directly with Gainsco's marketing efforts' creates a contradiction. The original text established the sponsor's approval rights and protection against other sponsors receiving greater benefits unless they paid more. The changed text permits Racing to give greater value benefits based on a subjective assessment of 'direct conflict' with Gainsco's marketing efforts, undermining the sponsor's approval right and benefit protection clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Sponsor acknowledges that Racing has arranged and may arrange in the future for other sponsors for the Racing Team. Racing agrees that, during the term of this Agreement, (i) Sponsor shall have the right to approve or disapprove any additional sponsor identified by Racing, and (ii) Racing can secure benefits of greater value to other sponsors as long as the additional sponsorship does not conflict directly with Gainsco's marketing efforts. Unless another proposed sponsor has agreed to pay a sponsorship fee that exceeds the amount paid by Sponsor, no other sponsor shall receive any benefit of greater value (including either an equivalent or a more prominent use of another sponsor's name, logo or other identifying information) than the Benefits provided to the Sponsor hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by allowing Racing to provide greater benefits to other sponsors as long as it doesn't 'directly conflict' with the Sponsor's marketing, which undermines the Sponsor's approval rights and benefit protection, as established by the original text and the contradicted section. The original text stated Sponsor shall have the right to approve or disapprove any additional sponsor identified by Racing, and that another sponsor would need to pay more to get better benefits, whereas the new text introduces an ambiguous exception based on Racing's subjective evaluation, creating a clear in-text contradiction related to conflicting obligations/rights.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 1 hereof, the Sponsor shall have the right at any time prior to December 31, 2010 to terminate this Agreement by giving written notice of such termination to Racing. In the event of such a termination, (i) the Sponsor shall have no further obligation to make payments toward the sponsorship fee contemplated in Section 3 hereof, (ii) Racing shall have no further obligation to provide any Benefits hereunder, and (iii) the remaining provisions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 1 hereof, the Sponsor shall have the right at any time prior to December 31, 2010 to terminate this Agreement by giving written notice of such termination to Racing. In the event of such a termination, (i) the Sponsor shall have no further obligation to make payments toward the sponsorship fee contemplated in Section 3 hereof, except in the event of gross negligence on the part of the sponsor. (ii) Racing shall have no further obligation to provide any Benefits hereunder, and (iii) the remaining provisions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to Section 14, specifically adding 'except in the event of gross negligence on the part of the sponsor' creates a contradiction. The original text unconditionally released the Sponsor from payment obligations upon termination, regardless of reason. However, the changed version introduces gross negligence as a condition where payment obligations might still exist, which means that in the event of termination due to 'gross negligence' the sponsor will be liable for payment. This is contrary to the original intention of no obligations upon termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Sponsor shall pay to Racing a sponsorship fee in the amount of $750,000.00 for the term of this Agreement, payable in an initial installment payable on or before February 1, 2010 in the amount of $350,000.00 and ten installments of $40,000.00 on or before the first day of each month commencing March 1, 2010 and ending with the installment due on December 1, 2010 (unless this Agreement is sooner terminated pursuant to Section 14 hereof, in which case Sponsor shall have no obligation to make any payments after the date of termination).
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 14 stated that upon termination, the Sponsor had no further obligation to make payments. The modified text introduces an exception, stating that the Sponsor *does* have an obligation to make payments if the termination is due to 'gross negligence'. This directly contradicts the language in the Contradicted Text (Section 3), which states: "(unless this Agreement is sooner terminated pursuant to Section 14 hereof, in which case Sponsor shall have no obligation to make any payments after the date of termination)". The addition of 'except in the event of gross negligence' creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction because Section 3 explicitly states no obligation after termination per Section 14, which is now conditional.
